title: Physics defaults (gravity 0.15 / maxWind 10) hand-copied across 5 sites incl. a client file that already imports GRAVITY
evidence: create_room writes `maxWind: ...? : 10, gravity: ...? : 0.15`; restart_game repeats the identical literal default-fill in BOTH fetchRematchInfo (37-38) and the build path (181-182). These are the SAME determinism-critical defaults that live as GRAVITY=0.15 / MAX_WIND=10 in shared/src/engine/Physics.ts:19,25. Worse, NetworkClient.ts:673-674 also hardcodes the `10`/`0.15` literals in the rematch-options projection EVEN THOUGH that very file imports GRAVITY from @shared/engine/Physics (line 9) and uses it correctly at line 186 — so the constant is in scope and was bypassed. Edge functions can't import shared/ (forced); the client site has no such excuse. Unlike CORS/STALE_MS/reaper (P3-14 consolidated into _shared/mod.ts), these physics defaults were never consolidated.
impact: Drift hazard: a tuning change to the canonical GRAVITY/MAX_WIND that updates only some copies (or only the engine) gives create_room, restart_game, and the client's rematch path divergent fallback physics. The fallback fires for legacy/malformed option rows; a desynced default seeds clients' engines with different gravity/wind caps, which in lockstep silently desyncs hot-seat-vs-networked or new-game-vs-rematch.
recommendation: Client site (NetworkClient.ts:673-674): use the already-imported GRAVITY and import MAX_WIND — no literals. Edge side: add `export const DEFAULT_GRAVITY = 0.15` / `DEFAULT_MAX_WIND = 10` to _shared/mod.ts with a 'MUST match shared/engine/Physics.ts' comment and replace the three literal sites (optionally a shared coercePhysicsDefaults helper).
acceptance_criteria: No literal 0.15 or :10 physics default remains in any edge */index.ts; all reference a _shared constant with a comment pinning it to shared/engine/Physics.ts

title: shared/src/index.ts barrel is dead code — zero importers; all consumers use deep @shared/* paths
evidence: shared/package.json exposes subpath exports `"./*": "./src/*"` (no `"."` root entry). Every client import uses the deep alias `from '@shared/engine/...'` / `'@shared/types/...'` (13 files, verified via grep — 0 import `from '@shared'`), and the scripts/checks/*.mjs harnesses import relative deep paths like `'../../shared/src/engine/Physics.ts'`. Nothing imports the barrel. The barrel also already DRIFTS from the real surface: it omits barrelTip, the net/ helpers (replayInChunks, shouldBufferSeq), and WeaponSystem items that ARE consumed via deep import.
impact: A 78-line file that purports to be 'the public surface of @singedterra/shared' but is unreachable and already out of sync. A maintainer editing it (e.g. to expose/rename an export) changes nothing observable and may believe they've changed the API; conversely deep-path consumers bypass any intended encapsulation. Pure cognitive-overhead / false-source-of-truth hazard.
recommendation: Either (a) delete shared/src/index.ts and accept deep-path imports as the contract, or (b) add a `"."` export to shared/package.json + a path alias and migrate consumers to the barrel, then lint-forbid deep imports. (a) is lower-effort given the existing convention.
acceptance_criteria: No file in the repo purports to be the shared public surface unless something imports it
If kept, at least one consumer imports from the barrel and a test/lint forbids drift

title: Dead Tank.create and Tank.bounds methods on the Tank helper object
evidence: The `Tank` const exposes create(), applyDamage(), bounds(). grep across client/shared/scripts shows only Tank.applyDamage is called (GameEngine.ts:1209). Tank.create has 0 callers (tanks are built via createTank()/placeTanks()), Tank.bounds has 0 callers (`.bounds(` matches nothing). The full TankState literal in Tank.create duplicates the field set that createTank() also constructs.
impact: Tank.create duplicates the TankState-construction field list that createTank() owns; when a TankState field is added (e.g. the recent shieldHp/buried/kills), the dead copy silently goes stale, and anyone who later wires it up gets a malformed tank. Low severity (unreachable today) but a latent drift trap on the core entity shape.
recommendation: Delete Tank.create and Tank.bounds, or fold the lone surviving method (applyDamage) into a named export and drop the Tank namespace object.
acceptance_criteria: No exported function/method in Tank.ts lacks a caller
TankState is constructed in exactly one place

title: GameEngine.clone() hand-enumerates all 23 private fields + every GameState property — silent-desync trap when a field is added
evidence: clone() builds a bare instance via Object.create(GameEngine.prototype) and then manually copies each of the 23 private fields (state, terrain, explosionSeq, fire, fireDef, fireCenter, fireScorched, shooterId, shotDamage, windRng/Seed/Calls, maxWind, gravity, seed, totalRounds, interestRate, suddenDeathTurn, turnAtRoundStart, armsLevel, pendingSettle, surfaceCache/Version) AND re-lists every GameState property (lines 503-520). All 23 are currently handled correctly. The ONLY caller is NetworkClient.ts:787 (`this.engine.clone()`), used to derive the next active seat for the lockstep referee.
impact: Concrete defect class: any new private mutable field added to this 1533-LOC engine that is not ALSO added to clone() yields a clone that silently diverges from the original. Because the clone feeds the networked next-seat derivation, a missed field can desync the lockstep (wrong seat / wrong replayed outcome) with NO type error and NO local hot-seat symptom — exactly the determinism-duplication hazard CLAUDE.md warns about, but internal to the class. The growth to 23 fields makes the next field addition the dangerous edit, and a 90-line manual clone is the kind of code reviewers skim.
recommendation: Add a structural guard so clone completeness can't silently rot: either (a) a unit/harness assertion that round-trips a mutated engine through clone() and deep-equals all fields (Object.keys parity), or (b) drive clone() off a single FIELD list / serialize-deserialize so adding a field is impossible to forget. Even just a `// when you add a field here, add it to clone()` cross-reference comment on the field block reduces the trap.
acceptance_criteria: A check exists that fails if a GameEngine field is absent from clone() (key parity or serialize round-trip)
clone() and the field declarations reference each other so the coupling is discoverable

title: Accessory allowlist 'battery' hardcoded in referee (2 sites) duplicates engine AccessoryType — adding an accessory silently breaks networked play
evidence: WeaponSystem.ts:246 declares `export type AccessoryType = 'battery'` — the single source of truth, consumed by the engine's applyBuy (GameEngine.ts:679 `action.accessory === 'battery'`). The referee re-encodes this as a CLOSED literal allowlist in two Deno sites it cannot import shared/ into: validate.ts:117 (`const hasAccessory = action.accessory === 'battery'` → any other value yields a 400) and submit_action/index.ts:171 (`...(a.accessory === 'battery' ? { accessory: 'battery' } : {})` → a non-battery accessory is silently STRIPPED from the stored row). Both currently agree with the engine (one accessory exists).
impact: When a second accessory type is added to the engine's AccessoryType (a natural SE-parity extension — the comments call it 'currently battery'), the referee will (a) reject the new accessory buy with a 400 over the network, or (b) strip it from the committed action row, while hot-seat's engine accepts it. Result: a buy that works in hot-seat is rejected/corrupted in networked play — a hot-seat-vs-networked behavioral divergence with no compile error to catch it (the literal is on the Deno side of the boundary).
recommendation: Document the coupling explicitly: add a 'MUST match shared AccessoryType' comment at validate.ts:117 and index.ts:171, and centralise the allowlist as one `ACCESSORY_TYPES` Set in _shared/mod.ts so the two referee sites can't diverge from each other. Best-effort: a CI grep/test asserting the referee allowlist equals the keys of shared ACCESSORIES.
acceptance_criteria: The accessory allowlist exists in exactly one place on the Deno side and is referenced by both validate.ts and index.ts
A comment or test ties the referee allowlist to shared's AccessoryType so adding one flags the referee
